mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive in months when PAYEMS increases (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% imposes a restrictive discount rate and elevated corporate borrowing costs, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in both real retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals a coordinated contraction in demand and supply, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and INDPRO fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of joint-decline months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1400 thousand units reflects collapsing credit-sensitive activity that drags the S&P 500 lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST below 1400 during tightening cycles coincides with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 4.0% signals slackening labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending power and corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE exceeds 4.0% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of a high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and decelerating payroll growth (PAYEMS) captures a restrictive monetary policy hitting the real economy, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high FEDFUNDS and decelerating PAYEMS fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of relevant months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output and corporate earnings capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive changes in INDPRO coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter increase in real GDP (GDPC1) confirms broad-based aggregate demand expansion that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly increases in GDPC1 fail to coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of quarters over a 3-year rolling window.
